name: Tofu Pot Pie
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup Barley flour or unbleached white flour
1/2 cup Whole wheat flour or whole wheat pastry flour
1 cup Frozen green peas, corn kernels, or chopped celery (or combination of any of these equaling 1 cup)
1 package Extra-firm tofu, drained, patted dry and cut into cubes (10.5 oz or 16 oz)
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix together flours and salt. Stir in oil and mix in water. Chill dough while making filling.
2. In a large saucepan, bring vegetable broth to a boil over medium-high heat. Add potatoes, cover and cook for 5 minutes.
3. Add carrots, onions, and celery. Cover and cook for 3 minutes.
4. Add peas and/or corn and mushrooms. Cover and cook for 2 minutes or until tender.
5. Combine flour, pepper, and poultry seasoning. Add soy milk or water and beat together well.
6. Gradually add to vegetable mixture, stirring well. Over medium-high heat, stir constantly for about 3 minutes, or until mixture is thickened and bubbly.
7. Remove from heat, stir in tofu, and salt to taste.
8. To assemble: Heat oven to 350°F. Roll out dough to fit over the top of a 9-inch square casserole dish. Spoon tofu-vegetable mixture into casserole dish and cover with crust, cutting off any extra dough and folding over and pinching edges to seal. Cut 4 or 5 slits on top. Bake for about 40 minutes, or until crust is done.
